symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe lumbar pain at the moment of a heavy manual lift
symptoms.character: severe, deep, band-like across the low back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional onset with a felt pop is characteristic of an acute muscular/ligamentous strain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Felt pop / tearing sensation during the exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete pop at the point of strain suggests acute muscle/ligament fibre disruption.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe board-like bilateral paraspinal muscle spasm on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Protective paraspinal splinting is the hallmark of a severe soft-tissue lumbar injury — palpation is clinician-only and cannot be done remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to stand, bear weight or mobilise (floor-bound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects severity of the spasm; video/caregiver-observable and drives the need for in-person attendance rather than pointing to a specific diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worse with any movement, eased only by lying still supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical pattern (movement-provoked, rest-relieved) supports musculoskeletal rather than visceral or inflammatory pathology.
Renal colic is classically colicky and the patient cannot lie still; relief with stillness argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radicular leg pain, numbness or paraesthesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of radicular symptoms lowers the probability of nerve-root compression, though examination is still needed to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bladder/bowel control and no saddle anaesthesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative sphincter/saddle history is the key history-based screen for cauda equina — but only examination can formally exclude it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative SLR reduces the likelihood of a symptomatic disc herniation; cannot be performed over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric 2+ reflexes and preserved light-touch sensation in the lower limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact lower-limb neurology argues against cord/cauda-equina compression — an examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: No midline bony step-off and no spinous-process tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of midline bony tenderness/step-off makes a vertebral fracture unlikely; requires palpation.
Sympathetic response to severe pain — clinician-measured, no home device; a red herring that must not be mis-read as systemic illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanism — manual lift of a heavy non-weight-bearing resident without using the available hoist

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-load manual patient handling with poor mechanics is the direct causal exposure and the target for prevention.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Benign severe mechanical strain with intact neurology
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional onset plus severe paraspinal spasm with a fully intact neurological examination is the pattern that confirms benign soft-tissue strain and excludes a surgical spinal emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: No routine imaging (X-ray/MRI) at first presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Uncomplicated acute mechanical low back pain with a negative red-flag screen does not warrant imaging; reserve MRI for evolving neurological deficit or suspected serious pathology.
Indicated only if cauda-equina or a progressive neurological deficit develops (see escalation edges) — an in-person/emergency pathway, not available via telehealth.
